title: Conflux and AMSL Aero Collaborate on Hydrogen Fuel Cell Cooling for Long-Range Zero-Emission VTOL Aircraft
content: Conflux Technology, a leader in additive manufactured heat exchangers, has partnered with AMSL Aero to develop hydrogen fuel cell cooling for Vertiia, a long-range, zero-emissions electric VTOL aircraft capable of flying 1,000 km. Announced on March 26, 2025, in Melbourne, the collaboration has completed its first phase, designing innovative heat exchangers to optimize weight, volume, and thermal performance. The project is now in its second phase, focusing on a proof-of-concept assembly for Vertiiaâ€™s powertrain. This partnership advances sustainable aviation, leveraging Confluxâ€™s expertise in aerospace and additive manufacturing to enhance efficiency and performance.
author: Analysts Account
